sections.title: Fats
sections.narration: fat is 0.1g. omega 3 is doing serious work here. for a vegetable, the question is whether the payoff is worth the plate space.
sections.subtitleText: fat is 0.1g. omega 3 is doing serious work here. for a vegetable, the question is whether the payoff is worth the plate space.

sections.title: Carbs
sections.narration: carbs is 3g. glycemic index, fibre, and sugar are doing most of the work. for vegetables, low downside is useful, but there still needs to be enough nutritional return.
sections.subtitleText: carbs is 3g. glycemic index, fibre, and sugar are doing most of the work. for vegetables, low downside is useful, but there still needs to be enough nutritional return.

sections.title: Proteins
sections.narration: protein is 0.9g. protein quality is clearly one of the selling points. protein is not the main pitch here, but extra support still matters.
sections.subtitleText: protein is 0.9g. protein quality is clearly one of the selling points. protein is not the main pitch here, but extra support still matters.

sections.title: Vitamins
sections.narration: vitamin k at 17% DV, vitamin a at 4% DV, vitamin c at 3% DV stand out most. that is not enough to move the verdict very far on its own.
sections.subtitleText: vitamin k at 17% DV, vitamin a at 4% DV, vitamin c at 3% DV stand out most. that is not enough to move the verdict very far on its own.

sections.title: Minerals
sections.narration: potassium at 3% DV, magnesium at 1% DV, iron at 2% DV stand out most. that stays fairly limited overall.
sections.subtitleText: potassium at 3% DV, magnesium at 1% DV, iron at 2% DV stand out most. that stays fairly limited overall.

sections.title: Pros
sections.narration: the upsides first. adds hydrating volume because it can bulk out meals with almost no calories. easy salad base because very practical in sandwiches, burgers, and salads. mild and easy to tolerate because a practical plus for people who dislike bitter greens.

sections.title: Cons
sections.narration: on the weaker side. micronutrient payoff is weak because that is a major problem in a category meant to reward nutrient density. fibre payoff is weak for a vegetable because it does not offer much of the maintenance-and-repair value the category is supposed to reward. mostly water with limited nutritional return because very low payoff compared with stronger vegetables.

closing.summary: Iceberg Lettuce stands out for adds hydrating volume and easy salad base. but it also gets held back by micronutrient payoff is weak and fibre payoff is weak for a vegetable. Best only when you need a low-commitment add-on rather than a nutrient-dense anchor.

closing.cta: Would you rank it the same, or nah?
